main-modules/template-page/getting-start.title: Using Aether
main-modules/template-page/getting-start.content:
# Using Aether

# Using Aether

> Aether comes with some additional custom features
> 

## Left sidebar

The left hand sidebar, by default lists all the pages in your site and is wrapped in a synced block so that when you modify the content inside it will update throughout your site. This sidebar **will not** be visible on mobile devices so it is important to add page links to the mobile menu via the Super Navbar, learn more in the next step ↓

## Adding page links to mobile menu

Since the left sidebar will not be visible on mobile devices you need to manually add all your site pages to the links section inside a Super Navbar *(Site settings > Navbar > Navbar items)* so that they appear in a mobile menu. To make sure links don't appear in the navigation bar at the top, make sure to choose the **'Minimal'** Navbar type.

![Manually adding site links via a Super Navbar to appear on Mobile Menu](../../../Resources%20Overview/Exclusive%20Deals%20and%20Discounts/Using%20Aether/mobile-links.gif)

Manually adding site links via a Super Navbar to appear on Mobile Menu

## Right sidebar

The right hand sidebar, by default features a table of contents so users can navigate your pages with ease. The table of contents is wrapped in a synced block so when you modify the content inside it will update throughout your site. You can also delete the column, it would not break the template layout.

## Primary color

You can easily switch the primary color of the Aether template to suite your needs. To change it, head to your site settings (cog icon), into the **'Code'** page and inside the **'CSS'** tab, simply paste the snippet below and update the HEX color code to your liking. The primary color acts like an accent color and appears in places like code syntax.

```css
:root {
	—-color-primary: #1ca0f1 !important;
}
```

## Call to action button

Aether has an additional feature that lets you turn a link into a call to action button, check out the example below.

[**Call to action button**](https://super.so)

For a link to become a button it must be a single **bold** text block on its own line with a link. You can customise the appearance of the button using the code snippet below. To change it, head to your site settings (cog icon), into the **'Code'** page and inside the **'CSS'** tab, simply paste the snippet below and update the values to your liking. This will also affect the styles of the Navbar call to action button.

```css
:root {
	--cta-color-text: #FFFFFF!important;
	--cta-color-bg: #1CA0F2!important;
	--cta-padding: 12px 22px!important;
  --cta-border-radius: 50px!important;
}
```

![Adding a call to action button](../../../Resources%20Overview/Exclusive%20Deals%20and%20Discounts/Using%20Aether/call-to-action.gif)

Adding a call to action button

## Adding columns inside columns

You can now add multiple column blocks to Notion by using the Advanced blocks for multiple columns; type  `/2 columns` in Notion to add two columns.

## Custom site footer

Aether is compatible with the Super Footer. Add a footer to your site from the Footer menu on the Site dashboard in Super.

## Site footer social links

The site footer features a section to display social links, powered in Notion by a gallery block. By default the gallery block is loaded with a range of different Icons that are hidden. To view and enable them, hover over the gallery block and click filter button and uncheck the checkbox, this will show hidden icons. Then inside an icon update the link and check the 'show' box. Make sure to update the filter on the gallery block when you're finished.

![Updating footer social links](../../../Resources%20Overview/Exclusive%20Deals%20and%20Discounts/Using%20Aether/footer-social.gif)

Updating footer social links

## Adding new pages

Adding new pages in Aether is easy. Inside the **Control panel toggle block** on the home page of Aether in Notion is a **Create page button**. Click this button to generate a new page using the Aether template and then move it into the left sidebar synced block. This template page automatically has the correct layout and contains all the synced blocks required for the template to work correctly.

![Adding new pages](../../../Resources%20Overview/Exclusive%20Deals%20and%20Discounts/Using%20Aether/add-page.gif)

Adding new pages

## Content navigation footer

The content navigation footer is an optional section that you can see at the bottom of this section and it features two large buttons to help users navigate to the previous or next page. The links must be added manually and will only work correctly if it is placed at the very bottom of the center content column. To add this navigation footer to a page, head inside the **Control panel toggle block** on the home page of Aether in Notion and into the **tools page**. Inside is a **'Navigation footer'** template button that you can copy and paste into your other pages and simply click it ****to create a navigation footer.

![Adding a content navigation footer](../../../Resources%20Overview/Exclusive%20Deals%20and%20Discounts/Using%20Aether/180e6efd-14f3-4f4e-aa18-56777d28825d_(1).gif)

Adding a content navigation footer

← Previous

[Getting Started](/resources/getting-started-142f6d94771f814d98fcc5a7fad4ea16)

Next →

[Blocks](https://www.notion.so/8349bb5f568e415b8e952dc8a67eb81a?pvs=21)

main-modules/template-page/using-aether/.title: Documentation
main-modules/template-page/using-aether/.content:
# Documentation Getting started [Using Aether](/resources/using-aether-142f6d94771f81e7af91e7041c20766b) Use cases [Design System](/resources/design-system-142f6d94771f812e86a5cbde000787b2) # Documentation > Aether is perfect for building out documentation of all kinds, check out the content below for examples on what can be achieved. >
⚡ [Check out the official Super documentation built using Aether here](https://docs.super.so)
## Code Documentation ### Environment Variables Aether has built-in support for loading environment variables into the browser and Functions. Loading environment variables into Node.js requires a small code snippet. In development, Aether will load environment variables from a file named `.env.development`. For builds, it will load from `.env.production`. A `.env` file could look like: ``` Aether=https://dev.example.com/apiAPI_KEY=927349872349798 ``` To load these into Node.js, add the following code snippet to the top of your `Aether-config.js` file: ```jsx require("dotenv").config({ path: `.env.${process.env.NODE_ENV}`,}) ``` This loads `process.env.AETHER_API_URL` and `process.env.API_KEY` for use in `Aether-*.js` files and functions. For example, when configuring a plugin in `aether-config.js`: ```jsx require("dotenv").config({ path: `.env.${process.env.NODE_ENV}`, }) module.exports = { plugins: [ { resolve: `aether`, options: { apiKey: process.env.API_KEY, }, }, ], } ``` ### **Accessing Environment Variables in the browser.** By default, environment variables are only available in Node.js code and are not available in the browser as some variables should be kept secret and not exposed to anyone visiting the site. To expose a variable in the browser, you must preface its name with `AETHER_`. So `AETHER_API_URL` will be available in browser code but `API_KEY` will not. Variables are set when JavaScript is compiled so when the development server is started or you build your site. ```jsx import React, { useState, useEffect } from "react" function App() { const [data, setData] = useState() useEffect(async () => { const result = await fetch( `${process.env.AETHER_API_URL}/users` ).then(res => res.json()) setData(result.data) }) return (
    {data.map(user => (
  • {user.name}
    • ))}
) } export default App ``` ### **Add `.env*` files to .gitignore** Environment variable files should not be committed to Git as they often contain secrets which are not safe to add to Git. Instead, add `.env.*` to your `.gitignore` file and setup the environment variables manually locally. --- ## Reference Tables ### Light mode colors [Light Mode](Documentation/Light%20Mode%20142f6d94771f81798eb2e7c2f60ee14a.csv) ### Dark mode colors [Dark Mode](Documentation/Dark%20Mode%20142f6d94771f819caa58ce55feff7f8c.csv) --- ## Guides and Instructions ### Adding a custom domain to your site To add a domain to your site, first head into your site editor (cog icon) and click into the **"Domains"** page, then click the **"Add a custom domain"** button. After entering your own domain address in the popup, you'll then need to make some changes to the DNS settings through your hosting provider. You would need to do this through your domain registrar's (GoDaddy, Namecheap, Google Domains, etc) or DNS provider's (Cloudflare, Netlify, etc) website. For specific instructions, view the content inside the toggle blocks below. ## DNS Settings Below you can find the DNS settings that need to be included on your domain registrar's (GoDaddy, Namecheap, Google Domains, etc) or DNS provider's (Cloudflare, Netlify, etc) website. ### Root domain records For a root domain like `example.com` you'll need to add the following records: [Untitled](Documentation/Untitled%20142f6d94771f81438551c4f742948379.csv) ### Subdomain records For a subdomain like `blog.example.com` add this record: [Untitled](Documentation/Untitled%20142f6d94771f8102bdfed436ab95f3b2.csv) **If you run into issues, please make sure that you have:** - Added new DNS records to your domain provider - Removed old DNS records - Waited up to 24 hours for new settings to propagate
💡 This tool can be used to check if the records are set up correctly: [https://www.whatsmydns.net/](https://www.whatsmydns.net/)
← Previous [Blocks](https://www.notion.so/8349bb5f568e415b8e952dc8a67eb81a?pvs=21) Next → [Design System](https://www.notion.so/4518d74bace84b25a518e64497467506?pvs=21)

# Design System

> Aether is also perfect for design systems and guidelines
>

mindset-accountability/common-mistakes-0.title: Common Mistakes
mindset-accountability/common-mistakes-0.content:
# Common Mistakes

# Common Mistakes

---

After years of working in the agency space, these are the most common mental roadblocks stopping people from growing their agencies: 

1. Not focusing on revenue-generating tasks
2. Not taking the time to think
3. Zero urgency 
4. Settling for ‘just good enough’ 
5. Not putting in the reps
6. Struggling with chaos and control
7. Not doing what needs to be done
8. Playing the victim

## Get a detailed breakdown of each roadblock and how to fix them in this webinar:

[https://youtu.be/eHlaUYAE_i0](https://youtu.be/eHlaUYAE_i0)

mindset-accountability/developing-a-winn.title: Developing A Winning Mindset
mindset-accountability/developing-a-winn.content:
# Developing A Winning Mindset

# Developing A Winning Mindset

In sales, mindset is everything. It's not just about understanding your product or service; it’s about recognizing the value you bring and conveying that with confidence. Here’s how to cultivate the mindset that drives results:

## **Confidence is Key**

First and foremost, remember this: **you have something valuable to offer.** Your service solves real problems—problems your clients have likely struggled with for a long time.

- **Own this fact.** Confidence in what you offer directly translates into confidence on sales calls.
- You’re not begging for business; you’re providing solutions to real needs.

## **Know Your Worth**

Believe in the results you can deliver. If you have any doubts about the value you provide, those doubts will subtly show in your conversations and could turn prospects away.

- Reflect on your past successes, client testimonials, and the unique benefits of your service.
- When you’re uncertain, lean into the fact that you’re part of a supportive community ready to guide you and help you improve.

## **Stay Prospect-Focused**

A winning sales mindset isn’t just about closing deals—it’s about helping prospects achieve their goals.

- Shift your perspective from making sales to solving problems.
- This genuine approach not only feels more fulfilling but also builds trust, making clients more likely to choose you.

## **Lean On the Community**

If you ever feel stuck or doubt your abilities, remember that you’re not in this alone.

- Our community is here to provide support, share insights, and refine your approach.
- Whether it’s advice, strategies, or simply a confidence boost, we’ve got your back.

---

When you combine confidence, focus on your clients, and the support of a strong community, you’re unstoppable in sales.

mindset-accountability/eisenhower-priori.title: Eisenhower Priority Matrix Template
mindset-accountability/eisenhower-priori.content:
# Eisenhower Priority Matrix Template # Eisenhower Priority Matrix Template --- Sit down every evening and plan your tasks for the next day. This allows you to differentiate tasks based on their urgency and importance: 1. **Urgent and Important:** Tasks with immediate deadlines that significantly impact revenue or critical operations. 2. **Important but Not Urgent:** Tasks that contribute to long-term goals and revenue but don’t require immediate action. 3. **Urgent but Not Important:** Tasks that demand attention due to their urgency but have little long-term value or impact. 4. **Neither Urgent nor Important:** Low-priority tasks that offer minimal benefits and can often be delegated, automated, or eliminated. ---
🔑 The focus here is to prioritise tasks that will best allow me to achieve my goal. This invariably will be revenue generating tasks
📍 **Urgent & important**
--- - [ ]
📍 **Important but not urgent**
--- - [ ]
📍 **Urgent but not important**
--- - [ ]
📍 **Neither urgent nor important**
--- - [ ] ← Previous [The Perfectionist Trap](/resources/the-perfectionist-trap-bf49b0f520e6417b99da332b542186cc) Next → [Offers, Guarantees, Case Studies](/resources/offers-guarantees-case-studies-d73db88316ac4a0287f4eb8e86436bf6) On this page

mindset-accountability/goals-0042102c87a.title: Goals
mindset-accountability/goals-0042102c87a.content:
# Goals

# Goals

---

Goals aren’t just numbers you pick out of thin air because Twitter said “10K/m is the dream.” Your goals need to *matter* to you and align with where you actually want to go. You need to fully *understand* your goals, and then look at how to achieve them. 

## 1. **Daily Goals: Build Habits That Stack**

Daily goals are the non-negotiable actions you take every single day. These are about building habits that stack over time to create momentum and consistency. They’re less about results and more about disciplined execution.

**Example**

- Consistently making sure that you have your campaigns on and sending **at least** 450 cold emails per day beats “Get clients.”

## 2. **Short-Term Goals: Wins That Keep You Moving**

Short-term goals are milestones you aim to achieve within 1-3 months. They keep you focused, help measure progress, and give you a sense of achievement. These goals should be *specific, actionable, and realistic*.

**Example**

- Close 3 clients paying $2K/month within 90 days.
- Launch your agency’s website by the end of the month.

## 3. **Long-Term Goals: The Bigger Vision**

Long-term goals are the *why* behind your daily grind. These span 6-12 months (or longer) and represent the transformation you want to achieve in your business and life.

Long-term goals aren’t just numbers; they should reflect the life you want to live and the value you want to create. They’re the North Star, keeping you aligned with what truly matters

**Example**

- Hit $30K/m in revenue so I can hire a CSM and work fewer hours.
- Scale to $1M in annual revenue to invest in other ventures.

## 4. **Break Down the Inputs**

Big goals without clear inputs = daydreams. For any goal—daily, short-term, or long-term—you need to define the **inputs** that make it achievable.

**Goal:** Add $10K/m in revenue in 6 months.

- **Input:**
    - Build a prospect list of 500 qualified leads.
    - Send 100 emails/day.
    - Follow up with 3 personalized messages/day.
    - Book 10 sales calls/month.
    - Close 20% of the calls.

Break it down like this, and you’re no longer overwhelmed. You’re now working a system.

## Final Tip: Forget What Others Are Doing

If your goal doesn’t resonate with *you*, you’ll burn out chasing it. Be specific, keep it actionable, and always break it down into bite-sized steps. Now get back to it—success isn’t complicated. It’s disciplined.

mindset-accountability/learn-from-member.title: Learn from Member Mistakes
mindset-accountability/learn-from-member.content:
# Learn from Member Mistakes

# Learn from Member Mistakes

---

These are the reflections of how one of members struggled with building their agency, and how to fix them:

## **Entitlement**

My first mistake was being very entitled and having unrealistic expectations, without me realizing it. I went into this thinking I’d hit 10K/month within three months, despite having no prior experience. When I didn’t hit that goal, I felt like a total failure. I blamed this failure on two things: 

1. Myself. I wasn’t good enough, I wasn’t smart enough or I just didn’t have it in me. 
2. Everybody else. Lead Gen doesn’t work. Cold email is dead. I started looking for ‘proof’ that this reality was true, as this would excuse my own failure. 

## **Being Too Hard on Myself**

Feeling like a failure, I got stuck in a cycle of self-criticism. I had to get better, so I’d spend all my time working, or at least try to work. In reality, I spend a lot of time listening to podcasts or watching YouTube on lead gen, searching for the magic hack or secret trick. Constantly thinking and even dreaming about work exhausted my brain. In social settings, I was distant and moody. I found myself stuck in a harmful mindset: "I’ll enjoy life once I reach X goal." But with the unrealistic expectations I set, I was doomed to keep chasing a finish line that never came, leaving no room to enjoy the present. This tanked my ability to actually work on high-ROI tasks - leaving me constantly “working” but never actually getting anything done. 

## **Hesitation**

Fear of more failure made me hesitate to take action. I’d stop outreach, delay campaigns, and hold back on the work that really mattered. I was actively sabotaging my own growth.

Meanwhile, my friend, who started his agency just before me, kept pushing forward. A few months later, he's had triple the number of sales calls and twice as many clients as I have. Despite being in the same space for the same amount of time, he not only earned significantly more money but also gained valuable experience and clarity that I missed out on.

## **Comparison**

The thief of joy. Spending too much time on LinkedIn and Twitter, looking at people who were either full of it or had been in the game for years longer than me. All it did for me was feed my feelings of not being good enough, and made me even harder on my self. It's just noise.

# How to fix it

I stayed stuck in this cycle for way too long before realising things had to change. Here’s how you can avoid that: 

## **Build mental resilience.**

Everyone has that voice in their head that tells them they’re not good enough. You can’t stop it, but you can choose not to let it control you. Things like poor sleep, bad nutrition, alcohol, or drugs make it harder to fight those thoughts. That's why people feel shit when they're hungover.

## **Set specific goals.**

“I want to hit 10K/month” isn’t specific enough. Break it down into actionable steps and identify the inputs you need to get there. Find your north star and reverse-engineer the path.

## **Focus on daily priorities.**

Write down 3–5 tasks every day that directly grow your business. Once they’re done, give yourself permission to step away and do things you enjoy—work out, relax, and reset.

## **Understand growth comes in cycles.**

It’s not a marathon or a sprint—it’s like HIIT intervals. There are periods of intense work where you build, scale, and implement systems, followed by periods where you maintain, see how the newly built systems work, rest, and recharge.

## **Action silences doubt.**

When you focus on high-ROI tasks, take calls, onboard clients, and actually get shit done, all that mental noise quiets down.

mindset-accountability/the-perfectionist.title: The Perfectionist Trap
mindset-accountability/the-perfectionist.content:
# The Perfectionist Trap

# The Perfectionist Trap

Perfectionism is a common hurdle many of us face, constantly tweaking projects in the hope of achieving perfection. If not managed, it can be the death of your agency.

Fortune favour progression, not perfection. 

## **Input vs. Output**

To navigate through the perfectionist trap, I categorise my tasks based on their inputs (effort required) and outputs (potential payoff). This approach helps me prioritise tasks with the highest payoff, but identifying these tasks requires regular assessment of my activities.

## **Implementing the Eisenhower Matrix**

Every evening, I sit down to plan my next day using the Eisenhower matrix, which helps me differentiate tasks based on their urgency and importance:

1. **Urgent and Important:** Tasks with immediate deadlines that significantly impact revenue or critical operations.
2. **Important but Not Urgent:** Tasks that contribute to long-term goals and revenue but don’t require immediate action.
3. **Urgent but Not Important:** Tasks that demand attention due to their urgency but have little long-term value or impact.
4. **Neither Urgent nor Important:** Low-priority tasks that offer minimal benefits and can often be delegated, automated, or eliminated.

By categorising my tasks this way, I focus on what truly moves the needle – primarily tasks that are either "Urgent and Important" or "Important but Not Urgent." These are the tasks that typically drive revenue or significantly impact my goals.

The tasks in the last two categories – "Urgent but Not Important" and "Neither Urgent nor Important" – are ones I aim to delegate, outsource, or automate. This strategy not only improves my productivity but also helps avoid getting bogged down by perfectionism in tasks that don’t significantly contribute to my objectives.

## Eisenhower Priority Matrix Template

[Eisenhower Priority Matrix Template](/resources/eisenhower-priority-matrix-template-7ea907f8accf4259949a0bd208a8eff2)

## **A Shift in Focus**

This method has been instrumental in helping me shift my focus from trivial details, like obsessing over the perfect website background gradient, to actions that genuinely contribute to growth and success. 

If you find yourself caught in the perfectionist loop, consider applying the Eisenhower matrix to your workflow. It’s a straightforward yet powerful tool that can help you concentrate on what truly matters, streamline your workload, and sidestep the pitfalls of perfectionism.

offers-guarantees-case-studies/case-stud.title: Case Studies
offers-guarantees-case-studies/case-stud.content:
# Case Studies

# Case Studies

---

Social proof, such as testimonials and case studies, is the single most important aspect when selling anything. If you can showcase that you've fulfilled on your offer to previous clients, you'll effortlessly attract new business.

We must present our evidence in a way that is clear, quantifiable and outcome-focused.

## **Why Social Proof Matters**

1. **Builds Trust Quickly:** In a world full of options, trust is a scarce commodity. Social proof provides a shortcut to trust by showcasing that others have successfully used your product or service.
2. **Overcomes Skepticism:** Many customers are naturally skeptical about claims businesses make. Social proof, such as testimonials and case studies, can help overcome this skepticism by providing evidence from third-party sources.
3. **Creates a Sense of FOMO:** The Fear of Missing Out (FOMO) is a powerful motivator. Seeing others benefit from a product or service can spur prospects into action, not wanting to miss out on the perceived benefits.
4. **Validates Decision Making:** Social proof can reassure customers that they are making the right decision, reducing buyer’s remorse and increasing satisfaction with their purchase.

## **Types of Social Proof**

1. Interview Case Studies
2. Video Case Studies
3. Written Testimonials
4. Reviews and Ratings
5. Screenshots of results, booked meetings, client’s communication etc.
6. Endorsements

## **How to Present Your Social Proof**

When presenting social proof, it’s crucial to be specific. Clearly reveal who the client is to add credibility. Ask questions to allow your client to explain in detail what their current state was, before working with you. What kind of problems/bottlenecks were they facing? What have they tried before?

Then, you want to direct the conversation to having the client provide direct, quantifiable results from your work together—such as percentage increases, revenue generated, or measurable improvements—so prospects can easily see the tangible impact you’ve made.

This combination tells a story of the problems you solve, as well as it makes your social proof compelling and trustworthy.

### **Bad vs. Good Presentations:**

*We worked with X to increase website traffic vs. We worked with X to double the number of organic leads*

*We did email marketing for X and boosted sales vs. Our email marketing strategies helped X boost sales by 20% in 49 days*

*We booked 16 calls for X vs. We helped X increase revenue by 30% by generating them 16 calls in X days and helping them close more deals.*

## ***You NEED To ALWAYS Be Looking To Acquire Social Proof***

So many agencies deliver exceptional results and ROI for their clients, yet hesitate to gather social proof.

This is the lifeblood of your business, and therefore, collecting social proof is non-negotiable. Adding strong social proof to your website will instantly help you book more meetings, get less objections, be less dependent on guarantees and close more deals.

Simply ask: *“Hey {{name}}, I’m putting together case studies to attract new clients. Would you be open to a quick video interview about our work together?”*

Do not fall into the trap of putting it off or waiting for XYZ

If they decline, compile the results yourself, create a case study presentation using Gamma, record a walkthrough on Loom, and post it on YouTube with SEO optimization. Embed this on your landing page and use it as a sales asset.

Producing case studies should be a primary goal. It may take 3-6 months to build a strong case study, but every one of them significantly improves your booking and conversion rates. There’s no such thing as “too many case studies”—each one adds credibility and impact.

# Collecting Case Studies

[Collecting **Testimonials**](/resources/collecting-testimonials-468cfa10351244ebb160d7fd1a3a4f12)

# What to do If you don’t have case studies

[What to do if you don’t have case studies](/resources/what-to-do-if-you-don-t-have-case-studies-8156bd6e1906424798a0bad7eae2c1be)

offers-guarantees-case-studies/collectin.title: Collecting Testimonials
offers-guarantees-case-studies/collectin.content:
# Collecting Testimonials # Collecting **Testimonials** --- This comprehensive resource will provide you with insights, tips, tools, and a framework to gather persuasive testimonials and case studies effectively. (btw you could sell this as a framework to your clients 😉) ## **The Importance of Testimonials and Case Studies:** Testimonials and case studies are essential for building trust and credibility with potential clients. They showcase your success stories, demonstrate the value of your services, and provide social proof that you can deliver results. In today's competitive market, incorporating testimonials and case studies into your marketing strategy is crucial for driving client engagement and conversion. ## **Tips for Gathering Testimonials and Case Studies:** - Approach clients with gratitude and clear expectations: Reach out to your clients and express your appreciation for their business. Explain how their feedback can help your agency grow and provide a clear overview of what you'll be asking them. - Be specific with your questions: Ask targeted questions to elicit detailed responses. Focus on the problems your clients faced, the solutions you provided, and the results they achieved. - Make it easy for clients to provide feedback: Offer multiple channels for clients to share their testimonials and case studies. These can include email, phone calls, or video recordings - Consider using video testimonials: Video testimonials create a more personal and engaging experience. They can showcase your clients' emotions and authentic reactions, making them highly persuasive. ## **Tools to Help Gather Testimonials and Case Studies** 1. **Testimonial.to**: An easy-to-use platform that enables you to collect video testimonials from your clients. It offers customisable templates, automatic reminders, and seamless sharing options 2. **Calendly**: Schedule interviews with your clients for case studies using this convenient scheduling tool 3. **Google Forms**: Create custom forms to collect written testimonials or case study information from your clients 4. **Zoom**: Record video interviews or testimonials with your clients using this popular video conferencing tool ## A **Comprehensive Testimonial and Case Study Framework for Agencies** ### 1. **Framework Breakdown** 1. **Introduction**: Provide a brief overview of the client's background, their business, and the challenges they faced before working with your agency 2. **Problem**: Describe the specific issues the client was experiencing and how these problems were impacting their business 3. **Solution**: Detail the strategies and services your agency provided to address the client's challenges, highlighting any unique solutions or techniques employed 4. **Results**: Highlight the measurable outcomes and improvements the client experienced after working with your agency, using data and metrics to support your claims 5. **Testimonial or Quote**: Include a powerful quote from the client that summaries their satisfaction with your services and the impact on their business 6. **Conclusion**: Recap the key takeaways from the testimonial or case study and emphasize the value your agency brought to the client. ### **2. Exact framework with questions** 1. **Introduction:** Present a brief overview of the client's background, their business, and the challenges they faced before working with your lead generation agency. This sets the stage for the story you're about to tell
💡 **Example questions to gather information for the introduction:** - Can you provide a brief background of your business and your role within the company? - What were the main challenges you faced in generating leads before working with our agency?
2. **Problem:** Describe the specific issues the client was experiencing with lead generation and how these problems were impacting their business
💡 **Example questions to identify the problem:** - How did the lack of leads affect your business growth and sales? - Were you facing any difficulties with the quality of leads or lead nurturing processes?
3. **Solution:** Detail the strategies and services your lead generation agency provided to address the client's challenges. Explain your approach and highlight any unique solutions or techniques that you employed.
💡 **Example questions to understand the solution:** - What specific lead generation services and strategies did our agency provide for your business? - Were there any unique methods or tools that our agency used to address your challenges?
4. **Results:** Highlight the measurable outcomes and improvements the client experienced after working with your lead generation agency. Use data and metrics to support your claims.
💡 **Example questions to quantify the results:** - What improvements have you noticed in your lead generation process since working with our agency? - Can you share any specific metrics or data points that demonstrate the results achieved (e.g., increase in leads, conversion rate improvement, sales growth)?
5. **Testimonial or Quote:** Include a powerful quote from the client that summarizes their satisfaction with your services and the impact on their business
💡 **Example questions to gather testimonials or quotes:** - In your own words, can you describe the overall experience of working with our lead generation agency? - How would you describe the impact our services had on your business growth and success?
6. **Conclusion:** Recap the key takeaways from the testimonial or case study and emphasize the value your lead generation agency brought to the client. This is an opportunity to reinforce the positive outcomes and leave a lasting impression
💡 **Example questions to wrap up the conclusion:** - Based on your experience, what would you say sets our lead generation agency apart from others in the industry? - Would you recommend our services to other businesses looking for lead-generation solutions? Why?
By following this comprehensive framework and asking specific questions, you'll gather persuasive testimonials and case studies that showcase your lead generation agency's expertise and ability to deliver results. This will help build trust with potential clients and contribute to your agency's growth. ## Collecting Testimonials Templates [Video Testimonial Framwork](Collecting%20Testimonials/Video_Testimonial_Guide.docx) [Interview Testimonial Framework](Collecting%20Testimonials/Interview_Testimonial_Framework_(1).docx) ← Previous [Case Studies](/resources/case-studies-32635fdd5e874861bfe310ae7d322868) Next → [What to do if you don’t have case studies](/resources/what-to-do-if-you-don-t-have-case-studies-9a903ee9eba04947ae354b4acd9431db) On this page

offers-guarantees-case-studies/guarantee.title: Guarantees
offers-guarantees-case-studies/guarantee.content:
# Guarantees # Guarantees --- You want to make your offer so good that your prospects can't say no. To do that... You need to reduce the perceived risk for your prospects. The necessity of a guarantee depends on how good the rest of your offer is. If you’re just starting out with no case studies, or are operating in a competitive market, a guarantee can significantly improve your conversion rate. A good guarantee can take you from 5 to 15 calls per month alone. It cuts through the noise of empty promises, showcasing your confidence in your own offer. ## **Types of Guarantees** 1. Performance Basis - You pay after you get results 2. Implicit Guarantees - You pay as you get results 3. Money-Back Guarantee - You get refunded if you don't get the results 4. Free Trial Guarantee - You can ask for refund first 30-days 5. Free Work Guarantee - We work for free until we hit the promised results
💡 *We use guarantees as leverage to book sales calls. On the call, you might need to manage expectations.* ****
💡 *If you offer a guarantee, it emphasizes the importance of only taking on clients you’re confident you can deliver results for.*
# **Stipulations** When using guarantees, you must have stipulations in order for clients to be eligible for the guarantee. If not, you’ll never make money. There are generally 2 ways to do this: ## State Stipulations The client must already have something in order to qualify for the guarantee. ### **Examples of State Stipulations:** - *The Client must have a minimum of 10.000 subscribers to be eligible.* - *The Client must have a total TAM of at least 30.000+* ## Action Stipulations The client must do specific / multiple action items in order to qualify for the guarantee. ### **Examples of Action Stipulations:** - *The Client must allocate a minimum ad spend to be eligible.* - *The Client must create an industry-specific landing page with a VSL to be eligible.* ← Previous [Offers](/resources/offers-142f6d94771f81b59430eed17d6f3ed1) Next → [Case Studies](/resources/case-studies-32635fdd5e874861bfe310ae7d322868) On this page

offers-guarantees-case-studies/offers-14.title: Offers
offers-guarantees-case-studies/offers-14.content:
# Offers # Offers --- Your offer is not your skillset or service, but rather a claim of the benefits & end-results you can deliver. An offer is a clear proposal to provide a specific service under specific conditions. It outlines what you’re bringing to the table, sets expectations for the client, and explains the terms and results they can expect. A strong offer should be both attractive and transparent, helping potential clients immediately see the value. # **How to Truly Make a Great Offer** From our experience, people who struggle with their agency often do so because they don’t understand their offer. They stick to a "Surface Offer"—a simple statement that doesn’t provide enough detail or value to stand out in the market. ### *Example of an Surface Offer* “We get B2B SaaS companies on the phone with their ideal clients every month on a pay-per-performance basis.” This is known as a "Surface Offer" because it presents a basic solution but lacks in-depth detail.While this communicates the service, it misses critical elements that would make it more compelling. To succeed, you want to create a detailed offer that goes beyond the surface. ### *Example of a Detailed Offer:* “Our offer is tailored for [Sub-niche of B2B SaaS] companies in the US, delivering a minimum of 10 qualified leads per month through a pay-per-call model. We guarantee that the calls we generate will meet your qualifying criteria, charging only upon the successful qualification and attendance of the prospect. Additionally, we ensure a 100X ROI based on our clients' average contract value.” A detailed offer like this goes deeper, addressing the prospect’s specific needs and laying out terms that show confidence in delivering results. ## **How to Create a Detailed Offer** To create a detailed offer, think about what a prospect would want to know: - How will your offer meet their unique needs? - What specific benefits can they expect? - How are you managing their risks? ### Formula for Offer Creation: 1. WHO - Identify your niche. 2. WHERE - If location matters, specify it. 3. DO WHAT - State the result, such as solving a pain point, increasing sales, decreasing churn, increasing conversions, etc. 4. PAIN POINT - Address any challenges or limitations that other providers might overlook. 5. GUARANTEE/Risk Reversal - Add a guarantee or promise to take on the risk yourself. Combine these elements in a single sentence to create a concise and powerful offer: *“We help B2B SaaS companies in California get quality sales opportunities on a performance basis without relying on ads.”* # **The Thinking Behind Crafting an Irresistible Offer** An irresistible offer should be so compelling that prospects can’t say no. To do this, you need to take on as much risk as possible, while qualifying your prospects well to minimize any real risk on your side. This includes providing a significant ROI—aim for a 10x ROI for the client. When your offer stands out in the market, clients will come to you effortlessly. A few key principles to keep in mind: - Build competitive advantages and unique selling points into your offer. - Stack benefits that improve your offer without lowering your prices. - Aim to have the best offer in the market, not necessarily the cheapest. ### *Examples of Good Offers* Here are a few examples of compelling offers in various industries: - Lead Generation for B2B SaaS Companies - We flood B2B SaaS Companies’ calendars with meetings with qualified prospects on a pay-per-appointment basis - Sales Closing on Commission - Generate leads, nurture prospects, and close sales for a commission fee. - I’ll close your inbound leads and you only pay a % of the deals I close - Driving Leads for High-Ticket Coaching Programs - Generate leads and manage the sales pipeline for high-ticket coaching. - I drive my own leads and close high-ticket sales. You pay after the money is in your bank account - TikTok UGC Creation for E-commerce Stores - Produce user-generated content that boosts engagement and sales. - I’ll generate 60 TikTok videos that will 4x your following and generate you $X sales in the next 30 days # **Ways to Minimize Risk for Prospects** Here are several ways to structure your offer so that prospects feel minimal risk in working with you: 1. Offer a full refund if you don’t achieve the desired results within a specific timeframe. 2. Use a month-to-month agreement rather than a long-term contract. 3. Lower or eliminate setup fees. 4. Provide a performance guarantee, where payment is only made after you reach certain results. # How To Craft Your Offer Formula **The formula you should follow to craft your offer:**
**We help [WHO - your niche]**
**[WHERE - if it’s specific]**
**[DO WHAT - get rid of pain point/increase sales/decrease churn/increase conversion rate etc/get on more calls with qualified prospects etc]**
**[PAIN POINT - or something other people offer that’s detrimental to your prospects] in [TIMESPAN]**
**[GUARANTEE/Risk Reversal - If we don’t do it in {timespan}, we refund your money/we work for free etc.]**
**Combine all these elements in a single sentence now 👇** **Example:** We help B2B SaaS companies in California get quality sales opportunities on a performance basis without relying on ads. # Closing Arguments With a clear, structured approach to creating your offer, you can stand out in a competitive market and attract the clients you **want.** When you attract and qualify your prospects, there’s no real risk to you. Provide a huge ROI, 10x at least. If you do this, you'll get clients effortlessly. This is why competitive advantages/unique selling propositions are crucial. You build a few of them on top of each other... And that lets you improve your offer without upping your prices so you’re seen as much better than your competitors. The goal is to have the best offer in the market nobody else has. It doesn’t mean you need to be the cheapest on the market. It means you need to have the best offer on the market. Do this and you’ll be cashing $10k/m easily. ← Previous [Offers, Guarantees, Case Studies](/resources/offers-guarantees-case-studies-d73db88316ac4a0287f4eb8e86436bf6) Next → [Guarantees](/resources/guarantees-4f3598ecc0b847b9a3d84d75a6b64c02) On this page

offers-guarantees-case-studies/what-to-d.content:
# What to do if you don’t have case studies

# What to do if you don’t have case studies

---

Sometimes clients won’t go on camera, give a testimonial, or the relationship ends too soon to capture it. **Case studies are crucial, but they can be tough to secure.**

Enter the **“Joe Rogan Effect.”**

Film yourself in a podcast-style setup: camera at a 45-degree angle, mic in view, headphones on. Talk through the transformation you delivered with authority and professionalism—**no client needed! 🤯**

By highlighting the results you achieved, you can showcase your expertise and prove your value without ever relying on a client testimonial.

**Genius**.

## Step-by-Step

### Step 1: The Set-Up

Set the camera up so you’re speaking at 45 degrees - if you want to put a Mic or headphones on for a more authentic ‘Joe Rogan’ podcast look, then do this.

### Step 2: The Questions

All you do, or get your client to do, is to give answers, in a conversational style, to the below questions: 

- **Introduction- who you are, what your role is, what your company do?**
- **Setting the Scene (Before State) - who were your client and what were they trying do?**
- **Pain - What were they struggling with?**
- **Impact - What did that mean for their wider organisation (was it harming revenue goals, did it have a knock on impact for other stakeholders)**
- **Agitate - What other things had they considered/tried? Why had these not worked out**
- **Solution - What did you do? What was the process? Step by Step**
- **Outcome - List any quantifiable benefits of the future state.**

### Step 3: Tips

- Descript is a super easy-to-use video editor, it’s free, and you can edit your video like a Word document. It’s insanely easy to use.
- **Inves**t in sound if you can. EVERY iPhone can take great video. The biggest ROI in terms of video production is sound quality. A Rode Wireless Go Mic will cost you about £160 but will give you INSANELY good quality for your audio.

## **Example of a Finished Product For One Of Our Clients.**

[https://share.descript.com/view/of3WszxxFyX](https://share.descript.com/view/of3WszxxFyX)
